text.title: Customer Lifecycle and Real Examples
text.summary: Five-stage customer lifecycle with intervention triggers; SaaS email tool and developer membership examples with tier decisions and outcomes.

rule.statement: Instrument lifecycle stage transitions and trigger interventions at each: onboarding email Day 1, check-in Day 3, conversion offer Day 7 (for trials); usage-drop alert for at-risk; save offer at cancellation; win-back at Day 30 post-churn.
rule.rationale: Each lifecycle stage has a different intervention ROI. Day 1 onboarding email has the highest open rate of any email the user will ever receive (50–70%). At-risk outreach is cheaper than win-back.

list.item: Trial Day 1: welcome email + first value action prompt.
Trial Day 3: check-in email — did they complete the first action? Help if not.
Trial Day 7: conversion offer — limited-time or exclusive bonus for upgrading now.
Active monthly: usage report email — show them the value delivered this month.
At-risk (usage drop 50%+ week-over-week): proactive outreach, offer help session.
Cancel request: save offer (discount, pause, downgrade) before confirming cancellation.
Churned Day 30: win-back campaign — announce new feature or offer re-entry discount.

example.note: Three-tier usage-based pricing; 115% NRR achieved through expansion (users growing their contact lists).
example.code:
Tiers: Starter $19/mo: 1K contacts, 10K emails/mo Growth $49/mo: 5K contacts, 50K emails/mo (40% of users) Pro $99/mo: 15K contacts, 150K emails/mo
Outcomes: 40% choose Growth (anchor tier) Net Revenue Retention: 115% Monthly churn rate: 3% (Expansion from contact list growth offsets churn)

example.note: Freemium + two paid tiers; small VIP tier generates outsized revenue share.
example.code:
Tiers: Free $0: Public content only Member $29/mo: All courses + community VIP $99/mo: + Live sessions + mentorship
Outcomes: Free-to-paid conversion: 5% Annual retention (paid): 85% VIP = 2% of paid users, 15% of revenue (VIP LTV ~10x Member LTV)
